title: Lesson 3 — The Wellness Guru

words.definition: to leave hurriedly and secretly, typically to avoid detection or capture
words.example: The treasurer absconded with the funds before the audit began.

words.definition: high-sounding but with little real meaning; pompous
words.example: His bombastic speech promised the moon and delivered nothing.

words.definition: to persuade someone by sustained flattery or gentle coaxing
words.example: She cajoled her brother into covering her shift with promises of pizza.

words.definition: to express contempt for; to ridicule or mock
words.example: Critics derided the film as a shallow imitation of better ones.

words.definition: to drain of energy or vitality; to weaken
words.example: The relentless heat enervated the hikers within an hour.

words.definition: overused and therefore unoriginal and dull; clichéd
words.example: "Only time will tell" is a hackneyed way to end an essay.

words.definition: not feeling or showing emotion; expressionless
words.example: The judge stayed impassive as the verdict was read.
The mendacious salesman claimed the car had never been in an accident.

words.definition: just coming into existence and beginning to develop
words.example: The nascent startup had three employees and one customer.
The opulent ballroom glittered with gold leaf and crystal.

words.definition: having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way
words.example: The pernicious rumor spread quietly until it had ruined her reputation.

words.definition: not revealing one's thoughts readily; reserved
words.example: He was reticent about his past, changing the subject whenever it came up.

story: The self-styled guru threw an [[opulent|opulent]] gala to launch his [[nascent|nascent]] wellness empire, all chandeliers and gold leaf. His speeches were [[bombastic|bombastic]], booming with grand words that meant almost nothing. He would [[cajole|cajole]] wealthy guests with honeyed compliments, then quietly [[deride|deride]] them the moment they left the room. Skeptics who dared question his miracle tonics he dismissed as [[reticent|reticent]] cowards, though they were merely careful people. His pitch leaned on [[hackneyed|hackneyed]] slogans — the tired clichés you find on cheap posters — and on [[mendacious|mendacious]] claims no honest doctor would endorse. A young reporter sat [[impassive|impassive]], her face betraying nothing, as she quietly gathered her proof. The scandal would [[enervate|enervate]] the whole enterprise, draining its momentum within a week, and lay bare how [[pernicious|pernicious]] a charming lie can be. Before the story broke, of course, the guru had already [[abscond|absconded]] with the investors' cash.
